signals.headline: March Inflation Came In Below Forecasts at 2
signals.raw:
March Inflation Came In Below Forecasts at 2.2%** — South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026, undershooting the median forecast of 2.4%, largely due to government fuel price caps implemented for the first time in nearly 30 years.[4] However, economists warned that upside risks remain elevated given oil prices surging past $100 per barrel due to the Iran conflict.[4]

signals.headline: Bank of Korea Holds Rate Steady at 2
signals.raw: Bank of Korea Holds Rate Steady at 2.5%** — The central bank maintained its benchmark interest rate at 2.5% at its April meeting, as widely anticipated.[2][6] The Policy Board expects inflation to rise to the mid to upper two-percent range for the year.[6]
